page: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based server belonging to the Dpldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 3780 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 10% for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Database and web serving capabilities are also strong, with top-tier results in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and PassMark database operations. Conversely, single-core performance is moderate, falling into the average category for single-core stress-ng, Geekbench single-core, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression.

The D64plds_v6 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades high memory capacity for dense, dedicated ARM compute cores and substantial local storage. This design provides qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that do not require large memory footprints, such as software compilation, web serving, and database operations. However, memory-intensive applications or single-threaded tasks may face bottlenecks due to the 2.0 GB per core memory allocation and average single-core performance. It is best suited for multi-threaded compilation, high-throughput web serving, Redis deployments, and parallel database processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based server from the Dpldsv6 family, utilizing 64 dedicated physical cores of the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor clocked at 3.4 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 3780 GB of local storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory allocations. It is well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based server featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based server with 64 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered compute server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz
128.0 GB RAM providing 2.0 GB of memory per core
3780 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Average single-core performance and low memory-to-core ratio as key tradeoffs
Suited for multi-threaded software compilation, Redis, and web serving
